title: pelargonium × asperum
intro: Pelargonium × asperum, also known as Zulu Geranium, is a perennial plant species in the geranium family, native to South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which make it effective in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Pelargonium × asperum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and soothe digestive issues. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various conditions, including colds, flu, and digestive problems.
